refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into two key sub-functions: acquiring timely data from the worksite and processing that data efficiently to update the model.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure latency separately for data acquisition and data processing stages under representative operational scenarios to ensure combined latency meets p95 ≤ 1.0 s.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model with minimal latency

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-bandwidth, low-latency telemetry links for data acquisition
DP2: Implement real-time data processing algorithms optimized for low latency
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP2 combined to address acquisition and processing latency

potential_sources: Data acquisition and processing are sequential but modular functions
Interfaces between telemetry and processing modules are well-defined
coupling_check.notes: The DPs are logically decoupled with clear interface boundaries, minimizing coupling risks.

assumptions_risks: Assumes telemetry infrastructure supports required bandwidth and latency
Processing algorithms can be optimized without sacrificing accuracy
Risk of telemetry link degradation impacting latency
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor telemetry performance and processing efficiency during integration to validate assumptions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for integrating telemetry data acquisition and real-time processing to update the worksite model

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data streams from vehicles and sensors

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data transmission between vehicles, teleoperation, and safety services

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes data acquisition and processing, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment.
Limited containment; vehicle mainly provides raw data, processing is external.
Primarily consumes processed data; minimal role in update latency.
Safety alerts are downstream; no direct role in update latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing update latency functions within NGHE Solution module reduces interface complexity and latency risks.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module is overloaded
Dependency on network link reliability for telemetry data
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider load balancing within NGHE Solution and redundancy in network links to mitigate risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement clearly decomposes latency requirement into acquisition and processing sub-functions; selected DPs logically address these with low coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion as update latency functions are centralized in NGHE Solution
Network link dependency is a known risk but managed via interface design
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution as the best architectural boundary for these DPs.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end and at module interfaces using telemetry and network monitoring tools.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of acquisition and processing functions to NGHE Solution module.

recommended_revisions: Monitor network link performance closely during integration
Validate processing algorithm latency under load

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment satisfy the latency requirement with clear architectural boundaries and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model with minimal latency

revise.why: Current level is sufficiently detailed and consistent; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Telemetry infrastructure meets bandwidth and latency needs
Processing algorithms can be optimized for real-time performance
open_risks: Network link reliability impacting telemetry data timeliness
